slides.title: Introduction to CNNs
slides.content:
Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are a class of deep neural networks, most commonly applied to analyze visual imagery. 
- Widely used in image and video recognition, recommender systems, and natural language processing.

slides.title: CNN Architecture: An Overview
slides.content:
- Input Layer: Takes raw image data.
- Convolutional Layer: Applies filters to detect features.
- Pooling Layer: Reduces spatial dimensions, retaining important features.
- Fully Connected Layer: Combines features for prediction.

slides.title: How Convolution Works
slides.content:
Convolutional layers apply a set of filters which activate when they detect specific features.
- Filter slides across the input.
- Each region produces a single output value.

visual_assets.code:
# Sample convolution operation in Python
import numpy as np
image = np.array([[1, 2, 0], [4, 3, 1], [1, 2, 2]])
filter = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
output = np.multiply(image[:2, :2], filter).sum()
visual_assets.caption: Convolution in Python

slides.title: Why Use CNNs?
slides.content:
CNNs have proven to be successful in many fields due to their:
- Translation Invariance
- Automatic Feature Extraction
- Parameter Sharing
- Hierarchical Feature Learning

slides.title: Real-world Applications of CNNs
slides.content:
CNNs are integral to various innovative technologies:
- Self-driving Cars: Image and video analysis for navigation.
- Facial Recognition: Identify individuals in images or videos.
- Healthcare: Analyze medical imagery for diagnosis.

slides.title: Evaluating CNNs
slides.content:
Key metrics include:
- Accuracy: Percentage of correct predictions.
- Recall: Ability to find all relevant cases.
- Precision: The ratio of true positive observations.

visual_assets.code:
# Sample evaluation of a CNN model
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score
preds = [0, 1, 1, 0]
true = [0, 1, 0, 0]
accuracy = accuracy_score(true, preds)
visual_assets.caption: CNN Evaluation with Scikit-learn

slides.title: Conclusion and Future of CNNs
slides.content:
CNNs continue to evolve, with research focusing on making models:
- More Efficient: In terms of computational power.
- More Accurate: Through better architectures and training methods.
- More General: Capable of understanding diverse data types beyond images.
